description: De Gaulles Streamlining OfFrenchArmytoContinue By OMER ANDERSON BONN NANA General Charles De Gaulle has informed West Germany that he intends to go ahead with the cutting back of the French armed forces from just over one million men to about 650000 The Germans asked De Gaullel to cancel his military cutback in deference to the uproar over pos sible US troop withdrawals as a result of the spectacular success of Operation Big Lift the airlift ing of the 14500 troops of the Second Armored Division from Texas to West Germany But De Gaulle rejected the German plea outright it is larned De Gaulle is cutting back French armed forces to save money for the nuclear reorgani zation of Frances defense estab lishment De Gaulle is discarding enfirely the concept of the con ventional mass fighting force Instead he intends that vitru ally all of the 650000 men re tained in the French forces will be highlytrained specialists He rejects entirely the warfare es calation strategy of the Kennedy administration whereby initial defense against aggression would be introduced only as a last re sort RUSK REPORT While in Bonn recently Secre tary of State Dean Rusk protested bitterly against the double
